sections.paragraph:
fut. יָהֹם i.q. הוּם pr. to put in motion, in Bewegung feßen; hence-

(1) to impel, to drive. Isa 28:28, הָמַם גִּלְגַּל עֶגְלָתֹו “he drives the wheels of his threshing wain.” (Comp. Arab. حَمَّ to urge on a beast.)

(2) to disturb, to put in commotion, to put to flight, e.g. when used of God, his enemies, Exo 14:24 23:27 Jos 10:10. Psa 144:6, שְׁלַח חִצֶּיךָ וּתְהֻמֵּם “send forth thine arrows, and put them (the enemies) to flight.” Psa 18:15. 2Ch 15:6, אֱלֹהִים הֲמָמָם בְּכָל־צָרָה “God disturbed them with every misery.” Hence-

(3) to destroy utterly, to make extinct, Deu 2:15 Est 9:24 (where it is joined with אִבֵּד). Jer 51:34 (with אָכַל).

[Derivative, pr.n. הֹומָם.]
